meta.scope: backend 01_source + frontend src; FE excluded from coverage; i18n mirror excluded from duplication

hotspot_review.method: each hotspot reviewed against its real code (skeptical default); per-hotspot code-grounded justification posted to SonarQube; no bulk-mark

dispositions.justification: random.random()*3.0 generates simulated response_time jitter (5.0 + random*3.0) fed into process_event inside a multi-round interaction-simulation loop (range(5) rounds over concepts). It is synthetic behavioral test data driving the learning model, never a token/key/nonce â€” no security or crypto use.

random.random()*0.3 generates a simulated confidence value (0.7 + random*0.3) for synthetic interaction payloads in the same J-over-time simulation loop. It is sampling/simulation input to the cognitive model, not a security-sensitive value.

random.shuffle(shuffled_nodes) permutes graph node labels to build a shuffled-DAG null/control topology (degree-preserving label permutation) for the transfer-learning statistical control experiment. It is scientific randomization, not generating any credential or identifier.

random.random() < edge_probability decides whether to add a random DAG edge when generating a null (IID) topology for the shuffled-DAG control; seedable via random.seed(seed) just above for replay. Pure Monte-Carlo graph generation for an experiment, not security-sensitive.

random.randint(0, i-1) picks a source node to enforce connectivity in the generated null topology (and random.uniform sets its weight). It is graph-construction randomization for the statistical control experiment, seedable for reproducibility â€” no token/key/nonce.

random.choice(medium_tasks) selects a learning task at random from the difficulty-filtered candidate list for an intermediate-mastery learner (curriculum task selection). It chooses pedagogical content, not a credential â€” no security relevance.
random.choice(tasks) is the fallback task pick when no medium-difficulty tasks exist for an intermediate learner. It selects which exercise to present, not anything auth/crypto-related.
random.choice(hard_tasks) selects a harder learning task for an advanced-mastery learner (difficulty>0.5 branch). It is curriculum content selection, with no security or cryptographic purpose.
random.choice(tasks) is the advanced-learner fallback task pick when no hard tasks are available. It picks which task to surface to the learner, not a token/key/session id.

rng.random() < p_correct simulates whether a synthetic learner answers correctly, where rng = random.Random(req.seed) (line 233) is an explicitly seeded RNG for reproducible autopilot simulation runs and p_correct comes from a Rasch/IRT sigmoid. It drives synthetic-attempt outcomes, not any credential â€” security-irrelevant.

`_rng_module.Random(step_seed).random() < _p` draws a Bernoulli for synthetic-learner IRT correctness (P=sigmoid(3*(mastery-difficulty+0.1))) in a replay-safe cohort simulation. The local seed (step_seed) makes outcomes deterministic for reproducible replay; this is ML/simulation sampling, not a token/key/nonce.

`random.random()` immediately follows `random.seed(42)` to build sequence1 inside the 'Test 1: RNG determinism' check of a replay-equivalence behavioral validation. It generates test data to assert seeded RNG reproducibility, not any security material.

`random.random()` after a second `random.seed(42)` builds sequence2 and is compared to sequence1 to confirm deterministic RNG. Pure determinism-test data in a validation routine â€” produces no token, key, or nonce.

`sum([random.random() for _ in range(3)])` after `random.seed(42)` computes total_reward for result_run1 in 'Test 4: Replay equivalence', then compared against an identically-seeded result_run2. Non-crypto replay-equivalence check, not security-sensitive.

`sum([random.random() for _ in range(3)])` after a matching `random.seed(42)` produces result_run2's total_reward, compared to result_run1 to assert replay equivalence. Deterministic test data, not a token/key/nonce.

`random.choice(["hcie", "dag", "random"])` assigns a simulated tournament policy mode for a synthetic interaction generator (Phase 8.2). It selects an experiment arm to drive ZPD difficulty, not any auth/crypto value.

`correct = random.random() < prob_success` draws a Bernoulli outcome from a gaussian-centered ZPD success probability to simulate whether a synthetic learner answers correctly. ML/simulation sampling, not security-sensitive.

`f"task_{random.randint(1000, 9999)}"` builds a cosmetic task identifier for a synthetic interaction record written to the demo store. It is a non-crypto display id, not a token/session/key â€” collisions only affect demo data, no auth role.

`random.choice(["hcie", "dag", "random"])` picks a simulated policy mode in a `/admin/metrics/test-interaction` endpoint that fabricates reward values to exercise Prometheus counters. Test/demo data only, not auth/crypto material.

`group = random.choice(groups)` (groups default ["hcie", "random"]) in assign_user_to_experiment buckets a user into an A/B experiment arm stored as experiment_group. Despite living in auth_service, the value is an experiment-assignment label, not a token/password/session id â€” non-security-sensitive randomized cohort allocation.

policy = random.choice(policies) in the 'random' branch of cohort policy assignment, sitting alongside 'seeded' and 'balanced' branches. It assigns an experiment policy to a synthetic cohort user (cohort_{id}_user_{i}) for research runs â€” experiment randomization, not security-sensitive.

random.random() < correctness_probability where correctness_probability = 0.7 + 0.3*current_mastery â€” a probabilistic-correctness draw for the deterministic-answer branch of the simulated ITS attempt outcome. The value gates whether is_correct is True/False for a learning-model interaction; it is never a token, key, or session id. Pure pedagogical sampling tied to mastery â€” not security-sensitive.

random.random() < correctness_probability (=0.2*current_mastery) â€” the low-mastery partial-credit branch producing is_correct for the simulated learner attempt. Output feeds interaction_data['correct'] for the knowledge-tracing model, not any credential or auth artifact. ML/sampling use, not crypto.

assigned_group = random.choice(groups) where groups defaults to ['hcie','random'] â€” uniform A/B experiment-arm assignment for a research experiment (comment explicitly notes 'can be enhanced with ML-based assignment'). Selects an experiment condition label, not a token/key/session id. Non-crypto experimental randomization.

_stdlib_random.choice(available_concepts) is the except-fallback for the 'random' baseline policy (primary path uses self.rng.get_bandit_stream().randint over concepts). It picks which concept/question to serve next under the uniform baseline policy â€” a pedagogical content choice, not a security value. ML baseline sampling, not crypto.

row = _random.choice(rows) is the non-deterministic-mode branch of the 'random' task selector (deterministic mode uses self.rng.get_noise_stream().randint). It chooses which candidate task row to present to the learner; the result is a content/task selection, never a token or key. Experimental baseline sampling, not security-sensitive.

row = _random.choice(rows) in the else (unknown selector) non-deterministic fallback, paralleling the deterministic self.rng.get_bandit_stream().randint path. It selects a candidate task row to serve, a pedagogical choice with no auth/crypto role. Non-crypto task sampling.

jitter = delay * jitter_amount * (random.random()*2 - 1) â€” symmetric retry-backoff jitter added to a computed delay before time.sleep. This is exactly the recommended thundering-herd mitigation; the value is a timing offset, never a token/nonce/key. Backoff jitter, not crypto.

correctness': random.random() < p_correct in the _archetype (novice/stable) fallback generator â€” synthesizes a correct/incorrect outcome for a SIMULATED learner in the contextual-bandit experiment environment. p_correct = compute_correctness_probability(mastery, difficulty). Generated experiment/demo data, not a security value.

_archetype_unstable fallback: correctness'=random.random()<p_correct and response_time'=random.uniform(2.0,20.0) â€” synthetic response and latency for a simulated unstable-learner archetype feeding the bandit environment. Simulation/seed data, never a token, key, or session id. Not security-sensitive.

_archetype_transfer_heavy fallback: correctness'=random.random()<p_correct and response_time'=random.uniform(3.0,8.0) â€” synthetic correctness/timing for a simulated transfer-heavy learner archetype in the experiment scheduler. Pure synthetic-data generation for ML simulation, not auth/crypto material.

_archetype_forgetting fallback: `random.random() < p_correct` draws synthetic correctness off a forgetting-decay mastery curve (and random.uniform(4.0,10.0) response time). Simulation of decaying learner performance, not security-sensitive randomness.

_archetype_exploration_sensitive fallback: `random.random() < p_correct` (and random.uniform(4.0,12.0)) produces synthetic correctness/latency for the spacing-benefit learner archetype. Experiment-data generation, no token/key/nonce derived from it.

_archetype_challenge_seeking fallback: `random.random() < p_correct` at difficulty 0.9 (and random.uniform(8.0,20.0) response time) generates synthetic correctness for the high-difficulty learner archetype. Monte-Carlo learner simulation, not crypto/auth.

Inside the distribution_type=='random' branch of an experiment cohort splitter: `random.choice(policies)` tallies how many synthetic users land on each A/B policy when an intentionally unequal distribution is requested. It selects a policy label for experiment-arm allocation counts â€” not a session id, token, or any auth secret. Non-crypto experiment sampling.

`f"http://{host}:3000"` builds the Grafana dashboard link where host = os.getenv('HCIE_OBSERVABILITY_HOST', 'localhost'); it is an internal observability-stack URL returned by an admin-gated endpoint (require_admin), defaulting to localhost and pointing at the co-located monitoring service. No application data is transmitted over this http URL â€” it is a navigational link to a local/internal dashboard.

`f"http://{host}:3000"` builds the Grafana dashboard link where host = os.getenv('HCIE_OBSERVABILITY_HOST', 'localhost'); it is an internal observability-stack URL returned by an admin-gated endpoint (require_admin), defaulting to localhost and pointing at the co-located monitoring service. No application data is transmitted over this http URL â€” it is a navigational link to a local/internal dashboard.

`f"http://{host}:9090"` is the Prometheus link in the same observability_links payload; host = os.getenv('HCIE_OBSERVABILITY_HOST', 'localhost'). Internal metrics-service URL for a local Docker/monitoring stack, returned only to admins â€” a dashboard link, not external plaintext data transmission.

`f"http://{host}:9090"` is the Prometheus link in the same observability_links payload; host = os.getenv('HCIE_OBSERVABILITY_HOST', 'localhost'). Internal metrics-service URL for a local Docker/monitoring stack, returned only to admins â€” a dashboard link, not external plaintext data transmission.

f"http://{host}:3100" is the Loki log-aggregation link, host defaults to 'localhost'. Port 3100 is Loki's internal HTTP endpoint; the value is returned as an internal observability dashboard link in the admin-gated endpoint, not used to send sensitive data externally over plaintext.

f"http://{host}:3100" is the Loki log-aggregation link, host defaults to 'localhost'. Port 3100 is Loki's internal HTTP endpoint; the value is returned as an internal observability dashboard link in the admin-gated endpoint, not used to send sensitive data externally over plaintext.

f"http://{host}:4040" is the Pyroscope profiling link, host defaults to 'localhost'. Port 4040 is Pyroscope's internal UI; this is an internal-infra observability dashboard link from the require_admin endpoint, not a plaintext channel carrying sensitive data to an external host.

f"http://{host}:4040" is the Pyroscope profiling link, host defaults to 'localhost'. Port 4040 is Pyroscope's internal UI; this is an internal-infra observability dashboard link from the require_admin endpoint, not a plaintext channel carrying sensitive data to an external host.

f"http://{host}:9093" is the Alertmanager link, host defaults to 'localhost'. Port 9093 is Alertmanager's internal UI; the URL is returned as an internal observability dashboard link in the admin-gated /observability/links endpoint, not used to send sensitive data over plaintext to an external host.

f"http://{host}:9093" is the Alertmanager link, host defaults to 'localhost'. Port 9093 is Alertmanager's internal UI; the URL is returned as an internal observability dashboard link in the admin-gated /observability/links endpoint, not used to send sensitive data over plaintext to an external host.

requests.get('http://grafana:3000/api/health', timeout=5) in _check_grafana â€” Grafana is an internal Docker Compose service name resolved on the container network, not an external host; this is an intra-cluster health probe (returns response.json() to status dashboard), no sensitive data leaves the Docker network.

requests.post('http://kafka-connect:8083/connectors', json=connector_config, timeout=10) â€” kafka-connect is an internal Docker service name; this registers a Debezium connector config on the cluster-internal Kafka Connect REST API, traffic never leaves the Docker network.

HealerConfig.prometheus_url default = os.getenv('PROMETHEUS_URL', 'http://prometheus:9090') â€” 'prometheus' is an internal Docker service name used by the self-healing worker to scrape the 'up' metric on the cluster network; no external/plaintext transit of real data.

requests.get('http://kafka-connect:8083/connectors', timeout=5) in check_kafka_connect â€” kafka-connect is an internal Docker service name; this is a liveness check (restarts container on 5xx/unreachable) confined to the Docker network, no sensitive payload.

schema_registry_url = 'http://schema-registry:8081' passed to CachedSchemaRegistryClient â€” 'schema-registry' is an internal Docker service name (file comment explicitly says 'pointing to INTERNAL Docker services', bootstrap.servers='kafka:9092'); Avro schema fetches stay on the Docker network.

schema_registry_url = 'http://schema-registry:8081' for CachedSchemaRegistryClient â€” 'schema-registry' is an internal Docker service name (adjacent code comment 'INTERNAL Docker services', bootstrap.servers='kafka:9092', redis host='redis'); cluster-internal Avro schema traffic only.

default_v3_base_url returns 'http://api:8000' under docker_env else 'http://localhost:8000' â€” both are internal: 'api' is the Docker service name and the alternate is localhost; worker-to-API calls stay inside the Docker network (function docstring documents exactly this Docker-vs-localhost behavior).

otlp_endpoint = os.getenv('OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT', 'http://otel-collector:4317') â€” 'otel-collector' is an internal Docker service name receiving OTLP spans/metrics over gRPC on the cluster network; telemetry export does not cross to an external plaintext host. (File is a dead '_broken' variant, further reducing impact.)

otlp_endpoint = os.getenv('OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT', 'http://otel-collector:4317') â€” 'otel-collector' is an internal Docker service name; OTLP trace export over the cluster network, no external plaintext data. (File is a dead '_old' variant.)

prometheus_endpoint = os.getenv('OTEL_EXPORTER_PROMETHEXPORT_ENDPOINT', 'http://otel-collector:8889') â€” 'otel-collector' is an internal Docker service name for the Prometheus metrics exporter; metrics scrape traffic stays on the Docker network, not external. (File is a dead '_old' variant.)

challenge: 0.15 + Math.random() * 0.1 synthesizes a jitter value for the attributionEvents array feeding a dashboard visualization panel. It is demo/display data for a chart, never a token/key/id â€” non-crypto UI mock value.
uncertainty: 0.08 + Math.random() * 0.08 fabricates an uncertainty figure for the same attributionEvents demo array rendered in the learner dashboard. Pure UI/chart mock data, not security-sensitive.
zpd: 0.05 + Math.random() * 0.05 generates a ZPD display value for the attributionEvents dashboard mock. It drives a visualization only â€” no auth/crypto role.
transfer_prospective: 0.02 + Math.random() * 0.04 produces a prospective-transfer demo value for the attributionEvents chart array. Display mock data, not a credential or identifier.

Math.random() > 0.4 is a coin-flip used only inside the if (isMock) legacy mock-grading branch (taskSignal?.correct ?? (Math.random() > 0.4)) to fake a correct/incorrect result when no real signal exists. Demo/test fallback, not security-sensitive.

Math.floor(Math.random() * 1e9) is only a uniqueness suffix for a throwaway demo account email (demo_${stamp}@hcie.test) in tryDemo(). It is a non-crypto display id â€” the account password is the hardcoded literal 'demo123456', so the random value is not a secret, token, or session id.

Math.random() in gaussian() = sqrt(-2*ln(Math.random()||1e-9))*cos(2*pi*Math.random()) is a Box-Muller normal draw for the client-side Thompson-sampling Beta demo (file comment: 'client-side sim; backend uses a deterministic RNG stream'). ML/statistical sampling, not crypto.

Math.random() in gaussian() = sqrt(-2*ln(Math.random()||1e-9))*cos(2*pi*Math.random()) is a Box-Muller normal draw for the client-side Thompson-sampling Beta demo (file comment: 'client-side sim; backend uses a deterministic RNG stream'). ML/statistical sampling, not crypto.

gammaSample()'s Math.pow(Math.random()||1e-9, 1/k) is the k<1 boost step of the Marsaglia-Tsang gamma sampler used to draw Beta samples for the Thompson-sampling demo arms. Pure ML sampling math, not auth/crypto.

const u = Math.random() is the acceptance-rejection uniform draw inside the gammaSample() Marsaglia-Tsang loop (compared against 1 - 0.0331*x^4 / log-acceptance) for the client-side Beta/Thompson demo. Statistical sampling, no security impact on tokens/keys.

`Math.random() < cur[pick].rate ? 1 : 0` inside pullN() generates a Bernoulli reward to simulate bandit-arm pulls for the Thompson-sampling demo (betaSample over alpha/beta arms, armSource 'demo'). It is a Monte Carlo simulation of reward, not a token/key/id â€” non-crypto sampling.

In pickTask(), Math.floor(Math.random() * tasks.length) selects a random task index only on the offline/demo fallback branch (taken when no deterministic `seed` is supplied). The output is an array index into the static K12_TASKS catalog used to show a practice item in demo mode â€” not a token, id, key, or any auth/crypto value.

In buildDemoMasteryMap(), `0.65 + Math.random() * 0.25` jitters a fake mastery value for K-2 concepts to simulate a 'mostly mastered' learner. The function's docstring states it builds a mock mastery map for demo/offline mode; the value is display-only synthetic data, not security-sensitive.

In buildDemoMasteryMap(), `0.30 + Math.random() * 0.35` generates a fake in-progress mastery value for K-5 demo concepts. Mock demo-data generation, not a token/key/nonce â€” no security impact possible from sampling jitter on synthetic learner state.
In buildDemoMasteryMap(), `0.05 + Math.random() * 0.15` produces a fake 'just started' mastery value for K-8 demo concepts. Synthetic demo data for the offline mastery map; not auth/crypto material.

In buildDemoTrajectory(), `Math.random() > 0.35` randomly decides whether a mock learning step is correct, to fabricate a demo learning history. Synthetic trajectory simulation (docstring: 'Build a mock JT trajectory ... for demo mode'); not a security value.

In buildDemoTrajectory(), `(Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.1` adds noise to a synthetic per-step JT value (0.35 + i/steps*0.4 + noise) to make the demo curve look organic. Cosmetic jitter on mock chart data; not crypto/auth.

In buildDemoTrajectory(), the correct-branch `0.02 + Math.random() * 0.04` computes a fake positive delta_m (mastery gain) for a mock learning step. Synthetic demo-trajectory data, not security-sensitive (line 1204 has two random calls; this is the correct-branch one).

In buildDemoTrajectory(), the correct-branch `0.02 + Math.random() * 0.04` computes a fake positive delta_m (mastery gain) for a mock learning step. Synthetic demo-trajectory data, not security-sensitive (line 1204 has two random calls; this is the correct-branch one).

In buildDemoTrajectory(), `0.12 + Math.random() * 0.10` fabricates a transfer_realized value on every 5th correct mock step. Synthetic demo-trajectory metric for offline visualization; not auth/crypto material.

generateRequestId() returns `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)}` as a client-side correlation id for ordering queued API requests in the RequestSequencer. Despite living under lib/security/, this id is only used to label/track in-flight requests for sequencing â€” it is never used as a session id, auth token, CSRF token, or any credential, so weak randomness has no security consequence.
random.choice(concepts) for the synthetic "random" sequence-generator arm â€” selects which concept to emit in a simulated sequence. Non-security content selection, not a credential.
f-string internal observability URL (pyroscope/alertmanager/dozzle) at the deploy host for the ops console. Internal endpoint, not external cleartext.

Thousands-grouping regex /B(?=(d{3})+(?!d))/ runs on Math.trunc(n).toString() â€” a JS number's digit string (bounded ~16 chars, trusted, not attacker-controlled). No practical backtracking.

Standard email-format regex /^[^s@]+@[^s@]+.[^s@]+$/ on a length-limited client-side form field (validated before submit). Single non-nested ambiguity over disjoint classes, client-only â€” not an exploitable ReDoS.
Regex /^-?d+.?d*$/ is a numeric-format check â€” anchored with no overlapping quantifiers (d+ then optional . then d*), linear time. Input is a rendered cell value.
Regex /^-?d+.?d*$/ is a numeric-format check â€” anchored with no overlapping quantifiers (d+ then optional . then d*), linear time. Input is a rendered cell value.

Regex //+$/ (and //v3$/i) strips trailing slashes from a config URL â€” a single anchored + on "/", linear, no nested/overlapping quantifiers, so no catastrophic backtracking. Input is a trusted env/config URL.

Regex //+$/ (and //v3$/i) strips trailing slashes from a config URL â€” a single anchored + on "/", linear, no nested/overlapping quantifiers, so no catastrophic backtracking. Input is a trusted env/config URL.

notes: Reliability is C (not rounded): 44 bugs under SonarQube 26.6 MQR model.
Quality Gate ERROR is on NEW-code conditions only (new_violations, new_duplicated_lines) because this fresh scan has no baseline set; overall ratings stand independently.

sonar26 reclassifies the older Sonar's 87 hotspots: it reports 0 vulnerabilities and 114 security hotspots (all reviewed SAFE here). Security Review A is backed by the per-hotspot review in this file.
